dataset.content_type: mixed text — conversation logs, notes, and extracted facts

_metadata.note: Benchmarked on 10,000 documents averaging 2KB each. All engines configured with default settings unless noted. Each query latency is the median of 1000 random keyword queries.

benchmarks.description: High-performance full-text search engine library written in Rust, inspired by Apache Lucene
benchmarks.notes: Fastest indexing and query performance. Requires Rust FFI bindings for Python integration. Index is compact and efficient.

benchmarks.description: Full-text search extension for SQLite, built into the standard SQLite distribution
benchmarks.notes: Good balance of simplicity and performance. No external dependencies. Index stored alongside data in the same SQLite file. BM25 ranking built-in.
Easy to integrate in Python projects but significantly slower than native alternatives. High memory usage during indexing. No longer actively maintained.

benchmarks.description: Tiny but powerful in-memory full-text search engine for JavaScript
benchmarks.notes: In-memory only — entire index must fit in RAM. Good for small to medium datasets. Would require Node.js runtime or embedding in Electron app.

benchmarks.description: Hand-rolled inverted index using Python dictionaries and pickle serialization
benchmarks.notes: Built as a prototype for the legacy system. Very slow and memory-hungry. No ranking algorithm. Included for baseline comparison only.
